entries.name: Psychosis/schizophrenia — basis and dopaminergic pathways
entries.memo: **Mesolimbic** hyperactivity (positive symptoms); blocking D2 everywhere also affects nigrostriatal (EPS) and prolactin.

segments.label: Symptoms
segments.value: **positive** (delusions + hallucinations — **both** required for schizophrenia; disorganized speech/behavior, agitation), **negative** (affective flattening, anhedonia, apathy, social withdrawal, impaired attention), cognitive, aggressive, anxious-depressive. Onset in **adolescence** (even <13 years); cannabis/amphetamines/cocaine/alcohol exacerbate → role of **dopamine**.

segments.label: Dopaminergic receptors
segments.value: **D1-like** (D1, D5 — Gs, post-synaptic); **D2-like** (D2, D3, D4 — Gi, pre-synaptic/autoreceptors).

segments.label: Dopaminergic pathways (from the VTA)
segments.value: **mesocortical** (→ prefrontal cortex; ↓ DA → **negative symptoms**); **mesolimbic** (→ nucleus accumbens; ↑ DA → **positive symptoms**); **nigrostriatal** (substantia nigra → striatum; blockade → **Parkinson-like/extrapyramidal**); **tuberoinfundibular** (DA inhibits prolactin; blockade → **hyperprolactinemia**).

entries.name: Typical antipsychotics (1st generation / neuroleptics)
entries.memo: **Pure D2** antagonists: suppress delusions but cause **EPS** and **irreversible tardive dyskinesias**.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **D2 receptor blockade** → antipsychotic effect (reduce positive symptoms, **do not cure**); "neurolepsis" (psychic and motor quiescence). **Phenothiazines** (chlorpromazine — the first, perphenazine, fluphenazine): strong H1 blockade, **moderate** D2 blockade, **sedative**. **Butyrophenones** (haloperidol — **GOLD STANDARD for positive symptoms**, high potency, strong D2 blockade, poor anti-H1 → little sedation; droperidol — antiemetic): **non-sedative**.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: D2 (+ anti-H1, anti-α1, anti-M1).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: D2 blockade **60-75% → antipsychotic effect**; **>75% → extrapyramidal symptoms**.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: schizophrenia (positive symptoms); **droperidol** antiemetic (vomiting from ↑ dopamine).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: like the TCAs (anti-H1/α1/M1); ⚠️ **extrapyramidal symptoms/Parkinson-like** (nigrostriatal D2 blockade: dystonia, dyskinesia, akathisia); ⚠️ **neuroleptic malignant syndrome** (more with butyrophenones); hyperprolactinemia. ⚠️ **worsen negative symptoms** (↓ mesocortical DA).

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: do not act on negative/cognitive symptoms.

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: dystonia treated with anticholinergics (DA/cholinergic balance). D2 antiemetics (metoclopramide/domperidone): see file A7.

entries.name: Atypical antipsychotics (2nd generation)
entries.memo: **D2 + 5-HT2A** → fewer EPS; the price: **metabolic syndrome** (weight, diabetes, lipids).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **5-HT2A antagonism + WEAK D2 blockade** ("atypia"). 5-HT2A blockade modulates dopamine: mesolimbic (↓ positive symptoms), nigrostriatal (↓ extrapyramidal), **mesocortical (↑ DA → improves negative symptoms)**. They also interact with all DA/5-HT receptors and α1/α2.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: 5-HT2A (+ weak D2, other DA/5-HT, α1/α2).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: N/A details in the documents.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: schizophrenia (**1st choice** per guidelines — fewer extrapyramidal/cognitive deficits); improve negative symptoms. **Risperidone** = the most used in **young patients** (disturbs other neurotransmissions less; 5-HT2A/2C/7 + D2 blockade). **Clozapine** = last-resort drug in **resistant patients** (the most effective; also acts on nicotinic and NMDA). *(Others: olanzapine, quetiapine, ziprasidone, sertindole, paliperidone, iloperidone, asenapine, lurasidone.)*

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **dysmetabolic/metabolic syndrome + weight gain** (especially 2nd gen); sedation (clozapine, quetiapine); ⚠️ **agranulocytosis** (clozapine 0.5-1%); ⚠️ QT prolongation.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: clozapine reserved for resistant patients due to its toxicity.

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: N/A specific.

entries.name: Aripiprazole (3rd generation)
entries.memo: **D2 partial agonist**: a dopaminergic 'thermostat' → few EPS and few metabolic effects.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **5-HT2** blockade + **D2 PARTIAL agonist** (antagonist in the presence of high concentrations of the full agonist, agonist in its absence).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: D2 (partial agonist); 5-HT2.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: N/A details in the documents.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: schizophrenia; **2nd choice in patients with Parkinson's**.

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: **reduces prolactin** (unlike the typicals).

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: new generation with **pleiotropic** activity (lurasidone/ziprasidone 5-HT1 partial antagonists; asenapine antagonist of all 5-HT + ↑ glutamate). **Sulpiride/amisulpride** = typicals, high-affinity D2/D3 antagonists (low doses), D1 at high doses.

entries.name: Antipsychotics — adverse effects and LAI formulations
entries.nameShort: Antipsychotics — adverse effects and LAI formulations
entries.memo: EPS/prolactin (typicals), metabolic (atypicals), clozapine = **agranulocytosis**; the **LAIs** combat non-adherence.

segments.label: Cross-cutting ADRs
segments.value: dystonic reactions (**macroglossia, torticollis** → anticholinergics); ⚠️ **NEUROLEPTIC MALIGNANT SYNDROME** ("lead-pipe"/cogwheel rigidity, hyperthermia, hypotension, tachycardia, ↑ creatinine/leukocytes → **DISCONTINUE the therapy**); sedation (clozapine, chlorpromazine, quetiapine); **weight gain/metabolic syndrome** (2nd gen); **hyperprolactinemia** (1st gen; aripiprazole reduces it); **agranulocytosis** (clozapine); seizures (clozapine); ⚠️ **QT prolongation** (most of them).

segments.label: LAI (Long-Acting Injection)
segments.value: sustained-release formulations (duration ~4 weeks) for adherence; ⚠️ **NOT at the first episode**. E.g.: flupentixol, fluphenazine, haloperidol, aripiprazole, olanzapine, risperidone. Advantages: less hospitalization/poisoning, better adherence.

entries.name: Parkinson's — therapeutic strategy
entries.memo: Too little dopamine, too much acetylcholine: replace DA, mimic it, do not degrade it, or **reduce ACh**.

segments.label: Objective
segments.value: ↑ nigrostriatal dopaminergic transmission via: precursor (**L-DOPA**), **dopamine agonists** (postsynaptic receptors), ↓ catabolism (**MAO-B and COMT inhibitors**).

segments.label: DA/ACh balance
segments.value: ↓ dopamine → relative ↑ of striatal **cholinergic** transmission → **tremor** → **antimuscarinics** are effective on tremor.

segments.label: Differential diagnosis with essential tremor
segments.value:
Parkinson's = **resting** tremor (disappears with movement); essential tremor = exacerbated by movement (noradrenergic discharge from the locus coeruleus) → **lipophilic β-blockers** (propranolol, metoprolol); ⚠️ L-DOPA **worsens** it.
• The strategy tends to **fail** as the neurodegeneration progresses.

entries.name: Levodopa (L-DOPA + carbidopa/benserazide)
entries.memo: Dopamine precursor + **carbidopa** (blocks peripheral conversion); after years → **on-off fluctuations and dyskinesias**.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **precursor/pro-drug** of dopamine (dopamine does not cross the BBB); converted by **DOPA-decarboxylase**. ⚠️ L-DOPA is **not lipophilic** → only 1-3% crosses the BBB → **always combined with a PERIPHERAL DOPA-decarboxylase inhibitor** (↑ central penetration + ↓ peripheral effects, dopamine = β1-agonist on the heart): **carbidopa** (IT, *Sinemet*) or **benserazide** (UK), which do not cross the BBB.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: residual dopaminergic neurons → D1/D2 receptors.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: absorption in the **jejunum** (⚠️ competes with amino acids → reduce the protein load); dysphagia in advanced stages. **Start with low doses** (minimum effective) to spare **D1** (direct pathway activated as late as possible). Wash-out: discontinuation every 2-3 weeks.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: Parkinson's (**gold standard**; great motor improvement).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **tardive dyskinesia** (80% long-term, dose-dependent, choreiform; more in young patients; D1/dynorphin pathway); ⚠️ **on/off phenomenon** (OFF = akinesia; ON = mobility with dyskinesia); initial **"honeymoon"** period (~1 year); nausea/vomiting, anorexia, hypotension, confusion, insomnia, nightmares; behavioral effects (**well-being, "dirty old man"**), **hallucinations/psychosis** (DA → serotonergic neurons → 5-HT); cardiovascular (peripheral β1); peptic ulcer.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: loss of efficacy over time (depletion of dopaminergic neurons).

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: **+ carbidopa/benserazide** (mandatory); **+ MAO-B/COMT inhibitors** (smooth out L-DOPA levels in advanced stages); L-DOPA-induced psychosis → **pimavanserin** (5-HT2A inverse agonist); behavioral disorder → **aripiprazole** (D2 partial agonist).

entries.name: Dopamine agonists
entries.memo: Fewer dyskinesias than L-DOPA, but the signature ADR = **impulse control disorders** (gambling, hypersexuality).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **directly** stimulate postsynaptic dopaminergic receptors (bypass the neuron). **Ergot** (ergot of rye): **bromocriptine** (D1-selective — as effective as L-DOPA but rapid motor fluctuations), pergolide. **Non-ergot**: **pramipexole** (D2/D3/D4 — advanced Parkinson's, levo-protective vs oxidative stress), **ropinirole** (D2/D1 — early stages, delays dyskinesias by preserving D1), rotigotine (transdermal patch), **apomorphine** (ALL DA receptors, sc with a pen — for **off** phenomena).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: dopaminergic receptors (variable selectivity).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: rotigotine **transdermal** (patch, stable plasma levels over 24 h); pergolide oral; apomorphine **sc**.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: Parkinson's (early: ropinirole; advanced: pramipexole; off: apomorphine).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: dyskinesia, nausea, sweating, hypotension; ⚠️ **pramipexole → impulse dyscontrol/gambling**; bromocriptine/pergolide (D1) → tardive dyskinesia.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: less effective than L-DOPA.

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: apomorphine when MAO/COMT are not feasible.

entries.name: MAO-B inhibitors (selegiline, rasagiline, safinamide)
entries.memo: Block dopamine degradation; **B-selective → no cheese effect** at therapeutic doses.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibition of **MAO-B** → ↓ dopamine catabolism → ↑ synaptic availability. **Disease-modifying** (slow the course). Selegiline/rasagiline = **antioxidant/neuroprotective/scavenger** effect on free radicals (early stages; keep L-DOPA low, avoiding desensitization).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: MAO-B.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: rasagiline 1 mg/day.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: Parkinson's (rasagiline slows progression, low incidence of effects; safinamide + dopamine mimetics).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ MAO-A blockade → noradrenaline accumulation (relevant in the cardiovascular patient). Cheese effect: see file B3.

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: add-on to L-DOPA.

entries.name: COMT inhibitors (entacapone, opicapone, tolcapone)
entries.memo: Do not waste L-DOPA in the periphery (always with it); **tolcapone is hepatotoxic**, orange urine.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibition of **catechol-O-methyltransferase** → ↑ availability of L-DOPA and its penetration across the BBB (fundamental for the **on/off** phenomenon).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: COMT.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: **entacapone** (effective but short half-life → 4-5 administrations/day); **opicapone** (very high affinity, more effective, longer half-life).

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: Parkinson's (on/off phenomenon, add-on to L-DOPA).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **tolcapone = highly hepatotoxic** (entacapone/opicapone manageable).

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: always in combination with L-DOPA.

entries.name: Amantadine, antimuscarinics and accessory drugs
entries.memo: Recycled antiviral + **NMDA antagonism**: useful especially for **L-DOPA-induced dyskinesias**.

segments.label: Amantadine
segments.value: **non-competitive NMDA antagonist** + facilitates dopamine release (via **DAT**) → dopamine mimetic; early stages; *Osmolex* (extended release, for the on; ADR = agitation).

segments.label: Antimuscarinics (benztropine)
segments.value: block muscarinic receptors → improve **rigidity and tremor** (little effect on bradykinesia, which is dopaminergic). ⚠️ NOT atropine (does not cross the BBB) nor scopolamine (sedative). Atropine-like ADRs (dry mouth, ↓ sweating, blurred vision, constipation).

segments.label: Accessory
segments.value: **pimavanserin** (5-HT2A inverse agonist → L-DOPA-induced psychosis/hallucinations); **droxidopa** (neurogenic orthostatic hypotension); **rivastigmine** (anticholinesterase → dementia in Parkinson's, see file B7).

entries.name: Alzheimer's — therapeutic strategy
entries.memo: Cholinergic deficiency + amyloid plaques; for now only **symptomatic**: ↑ acetylcholine and glutamate modulation.

segments.label: 3 approaches
segments.value:
① support **cholinergic transmission** (early stages); ② **NMDA antagonists** (limit glutamate/calcium excitotoxicity, advanced stages); ③ **NSAIDs/antioxidants/chelators** (↓ free radicals/damage).
• ⚠️ For the cholinergic approach, cholinergic agonists are **not** used (ACh is polar, does not cross the BBB) → **AChE inhibitors** are used (potentiate the ACh already released).

entries.name: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitors (donepezil, rivastigmine, galantamine)
entries.memo: **↑ acetylcholine** (donepezil): modest improvement; cholinergic ADRs (nausea, bradycardia, diarrhea).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibition (reversible/pseudo-irreversible) of **AChE** → ↑ persistence of ACh in the cleft → cognitive recovery. **FIRST LINE** (mild-moderate grade). **Donepezil** (Aricept): long duration (1/day), lipophilic (cerebral tropism), selective. **Rivastigmine** (Exelon): pseudo-irreversible, also inhibits **butyrylcholinesterase** (↓ APP/β-amyloid), 2/day or **transdermal patch**, better tolerated. **Galantamine**: natural, reversible inhibitor + **positive allosteric modulator of nicotinic receptors**; hepatic metabolism (CYP450).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: AChE (+ butyrylcholinesterase for rivastigmine; + nAChR for galantamine).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: donepezil oral 1/day; rivastigmine oral 2/day or transdermal; galantamine hepatic/renal.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: mild-moderate Alzheimer's (first line, therapeutic plan).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: from ↑ peripheral ACh → GI (↑ peristalsis, nausea, vomiting, ↑ acid secretion); ⚠️ **cardiac** (M2 → bradycardia/AV block → contraindicated in AV conduction abnormalities); ⚠️ **bronchoconstriction** (contraindicated in chronic respiratory diseases).

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: tacrine abandoned (hepatotoxicity).

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: synergy memantine + donepezil.

entries.name: Memantine
entries.memo: **NMDA** antagonist that blocks only the pathological **excess** of glutamate: for moderate-severe stages.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **non-competitive, voltage-dependent, moderate-affinity** antagonist of the **NMDA** receptor → blocks the calcium "leak" in dysfunctional channels (spares the functional ones) → ↓ glutamatergic excitotoxicity.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: NMDA receptor (site inside the channel).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: bioavailability ~100%, lipophilic; tmax 3-8h; ⚠️ **not metabolized** (excellent for the elderly) → renal elimination unchanged (caution in renal failure); half-life 60-100h.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: **moderate to severe** Alzheimer's (cognitive functions, behavioral/affective sphere).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: hallucinations, mental confusion, asthenia, motor slowing.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: poor long-term benefit; not reimbursed in Italy.

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: ⚠️ avoid other glutamate blockers (amantadine, dextromethorphan → psychosis); synergy with donepezil.

entries.name: Alzheimer's — accessory therapies and prospects
entries.memo: **Anti-amyloid** mAbs: remove the plaques, modest clinical benefit, risk of **ARIA** (edema/microhemorrhages).

segments.label: Accessory
segments.value: **NSAIDs** (ibuprofen, indomethacin, sulindac, tarenflurbil → ↓ central inflammation, preventive); **vitamin E** (antioxidant, classic adjunctive therapy); **statins** (↓ APOE4 → ↓ oxidative stress/amyloid deposition); **chelators** (deferoxamine → Fe/Cu; little usefulness in trials).

segments.label: Disease-modifying (experimental)
segments.value:
anti-amyloid → β/γ-secretase inhibitors (verubecestat, semagacestat), monoclonal Abs (bapineuzumab, solanezumab), anti-aggregant (tramiprosate), RAGE antagonists; anti-Tau → methylene blue, GSK3 inhibitors; nicotinic agonists; 5-HT6 antagonists; sulfated neurosteroids (PREG-S, DHEA-S); NGF vaccine.
## 🧠 MULTIPLE SCLEROSIS

entries.name: MS — basis and glucocorticoids (acute attack)
entries.memo: Autoimmune **demyelinating** disease; the **methylprednisolone bolus** suppresses the relapse but does not change the course.

segments.label: Pathogenesis
segments.value: **autoimmune** inflammation → demyelination → neurodegeneration. **T (↑ Th1, ↓ Th2) and B** lymphocytes cross the BBB → pro-inflammatory cytokines → attack on the myelin sheath.

segments.label: Synthetic glucocorticoids (ACUTE attack)
segments.value: **Mechanism** = inhibition of **NF-kB** → ↓ activation/survival/proliferation of T lymphocytes; lysis/redistribution of lymphocytes, ↓ chemotaxis/BBB crossing (immunosuppressive usefulness > anti-inflammatory). **Molecules:** prednisone→prednisolone, methylprednisone→methylprednisolone (oral), **dexamethasone** (more potent); IV in exacerbations.

segments.label: ADR (chronic use → hypercortisolism)
segments.value: ⚠️ acute attack only — hyperglycemia, myopathies, **osteoporosis/osteonecrosis**, peptic ulcer, cataract, psychosis/insomnia, hypertension/edema (mineralocorticoid activity). *(Glucocorticoid detail: see file A4.)*

entries.name: MS — non-specific immunosuppressants
entries.memo: Old-school broad-spectrum: **mitoxantrone** (cumulatively cardiotoxic, leukemogenic).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibit the **clonal expansion of lymphocytes** (enzymes/substrates of DNA replication) → act **downstream** (lymphocytes already activated/proliferating).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: DNA synthesis (purines/pyrimidines/folates).

segments.label: Molecules
segments.value: **azathioprine** (antimetabolite), **cladribine** (purine antimetabolite), **methotrexate** (antifolate), **cyclophosphamide** (alkylating agent), **teriflunomide** (pyrimidine synthesis inhibitor).

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: MS, rheumatoid arthritis, SLE, IBD (alternatives to chronic glucocorticoids).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **high toxicity** on rapidly proliferating cells: myelosuppression, mucositis, alopecia, cardiac/hepatic/CNS toxicity.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: non-specific action (reduces the immune response across the board).

segments.label: Drug Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: N/A specific.

entries.name: MS — disease-modifying treatments (DMT)
entries.memo: Modify the course: IFN-β, **fingolimod** (sequesters lymphocytes), **natalizumab** (risk of **PML** from JC virus), anti-CD20.

segments.label: Injectables
segments.value: recombinant **Interferon-β** (sc/im 3×/week → ↑ anti-inflammatory cytokines, ↓ diapedesis/adhesion molecules, ↑ NK CD56 bright; relapsing forms; usable in adolescents); **glatiramer acetate** (sc → mixture of amino acids that mimics myelin basic protein, diverts the immune attack; relapsing-remitting). **Monoclonal antibodies** (true disease modifiers): **anti-CD20** (ocrelizumab — also primary progressive; rituximab), **anti-CD52** (alemtuzumab — depletion+repopulation of T/B; ⚠️ autoimmunity: thyroid, ITP, nephropathies), **anti-integrin α4** (natalizumab — blocks lymphocyte passage across the BBB; ⚠️ **progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy/PML**). Mitoxantrone (IV, US only).

segments.label: Orals
segments.value: **fingolimod** (modulator of the **sphingosine-1-phosphate** receptor → sequesters lymphocytes in the lymph node; ⚠️ PML/fatal leukoencephalopathies); teriflunomide (↓ pyrimidine synthesis); **dimethyl fumarate** (via **Nrf2** → antioxidant genes, ↑ modulatory lymphocytes).

segments.label: Lines of therapy
segments.value: **1st** (safe, lower efficacy): glatiramer, interferons, teriflunomide, dimethyl fumarate; **2nd**: fingolimod, natalizumab; **3rd** (high efficacy, greater toxicity): natalizumab, alemtuzumab.

entries.name: MS — adjuvant (symptomatic) drugs
entries.memo: Symptomatic: **fampridine** (K⁺ blocker) for better walking, baclofen for spasticity.

segments.label: Antispastics
segments.value: **baclofen** (spinal **GABA-B** agonist → ↓ spasticity); **dalfampridine** (voltage-dependent **K⁺** channel blocker → facilitates transmission in the demyelinated neuron → ↑ motor capacity).

segments.label: Cannabinoids
segments.value: **Sativex** (THC + cannabidiol 1:1, transmucosal spray), bedrocan (phytocomplex), dronabinol/nabilone (synthetic) → **CB1** receptors (CNS, Gi → ↓ excitability/glutamate) and **CB2** (immune) → antispastic, analgesic (neuropathic pain), antioxidant.

segments.label: Others
segments.value: **tricyclic antidepressants** (descending inhibitory pathways → neuropathic pain); **cholinergics** (urinary disorders); steroids.

entries.name: Pain therapy — WHO ladder and adjuvants
entries.nameShort: Pain therapy — WHO ladder and adjuvants
entries.memo: 3-step ladder: **you climb, you don't skip**; non-opioids and adjuvants remain at every level.

segments.label: WHO Pain Ladder (3 steps)
segments.value: **mild → NSAIDs** (ketorolac — max 4-5 days due to gastric injury; paracetamol, metamizole/Novalgina, ASA, ibuprofen, diclofenac, indomethacin, nimesulide); **moderate → NSAIDs ± weak opioids** (codeine, dihydrocodeine, tramadol); **severe → strong opioids** (morphine = reference, oxycodone, buprenorphine, fentanyls, methadone, hydromorphone, tapentadol).

segments.label: Sparing effect
segments.value: combination to reduce the opioid dose by **25-35%** (by potentiating the descending inhibitory pathways / ↑ excitability threshold) → ↓ toxicity and ↓ tolerance.

segments.label: Adjuvants (NOT analgesics)
segments.value: **TCA/SSRI** (potentiate the descending serotonergic/noradrenergic pathways — *exam question*), BDZ, GABA-mimetics (**pregabalin, baclofen**), corticosteroids (block COX), **ketamine** (NMDA antagonist), anticonvulsants (carbamazepine, phenytoin, valproic acid, pregabalin → block Na⁺ channels).

segments.label: Objectives (chronic non-cancer pain)
segments.value: ↓ intensity, ↓ dysfunction, ↑ quality of life. **Subjective** assessment (1-10 scale); relevant **placebo/nocebo** effect.

entries.name: Opioidergic system — receptors and mechanism
entries.memo: **μ** receptor: analgesia, euphoria, **respiratory depression** and dependence travel together (Gi → ↓ cAMP).

segments.label: Endogenous opioids
segments.value:
**endorphins** (well-being), **enkephalins**, **dynorphins**.
• **Receptors** (all coupled to **Gi protein** → ↓ adenylate cyclase → **↓ cAMP**): **μ/MOP** (binds endorphins), **κ/KOP** (binds dynorphins), **δ/DOP** (binds enkephalins).

segments.label: Spinal action
segments.value: presynaptic (↓ cAMP → ↓ Ca²⁺ → ↓ exocytosis of pain mediators); postsynaptic (↑ **K⁺** conductance → hyperpolarization). **Supraspinal:** acts on the **GABAergic** interneuron → potentiates the descending inhibitory pathways.

segments.label: Subtypes
segments.value: **μ1** = **supraspinal** analgesia + euphoria (→ addiction); **μ2** = physical dependence + **respiratory depression** (↑ with lipophilicity → heroin/diacetylmorphine); **κ and δ** = **spinal** analgesia. ⚠️ κ does NOT produce supraspinal analgesia or well-being (rather **dysphoria**: ↓ dopamine VTA→accumbens). μ on the GABAergic interneuron → ↓ GABA → ↑ dopaminergic discharge (VTA→accumbens) → euphoria.

entries.name: Opioids — effects, tolerance and dependence
entries.memo: Overdose triad: **coma + respiratory depression + miosis**; tolerance to everything **except miosis and constipation**.

segments.label: Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: **constipation** (MOP intramural plexuses → ↓ ACh → ↓ peristalsis); **nausea/vomiting** (dopaminergic + serotonergic); ⚠️ **pinpoint miosis** (sign of overdose; mydriasis = poor prognosis); histamine release (hypotension, pruritus); **sedation**; ⚠️ **depression of the respiratory center** (↓ sensitivity to CO₂; ↑ with lipophilicity → heroin; cause of death in overdose); suppression of the HPA/HPG axis (amenorrhea); immune modulation (respiratory infections); dependence.

segments.label: Tolerance
segments.value: receptor uncoupling → **↑ cAMP** (biochemical marker) → ↑ dose. **Reversible** (~2 months of abstinence restore sensitivity → ⚠️ overdose risk in detoxified patients who resume their usual dose).

segments.label: Dependence/Addiction
segments.value: physical (withdrawal: agitation, cramps, tachycardia, tremors, sweating, dysphoria) + psychic (mesolimbic circuit/dopamine). **Craving** (↑ glutamate); **hyperkatifeia** (increased pain on cessation). Chronic relapsing disease.

entries.name: Weak opioids (codeine, tramadol) — Step 2
entries.memo: **Codeine = CYP2D6 prodrug** (→ morphine); **tramadol** is also an SNRI (serotonin syndrome, ↓ seizure threshold).

segments.label: Codeine
segments.value:
natural opioid (benzylisoquinoline); metabolized in the liver → **10% morphine**; poor lipophilicity (few central effects) → usable in **pediatrics**; often **+ paracetamol**; cough suppressant (+ dextromethorphan).
• **Tramadol** (Contramal): **MOP partial agonist** + **NA/5-HT reuptake inhibitor** (mixed opioid, TCA-like → potentiates the descending pathway); poor lipophilicity; complex metabolism (**CYP2D6**); used in IBD/rheumatic pain. ⚠️ **with antidepressants → serotonin syndrome**.

entries.name: Strong opioids (Step 3)
entries.memo: **Fentanyl 100× morphine**; **buprenorphine** partial agonist (ceiling effect, safer). Antidote: **naloxone**.

segments.label: Morphine
segments.value: **pure μ** agonist; poorly lipophilic; analgesic potency **reference**; **hepatic glucuronidation (UGT2B7)** → **M6-glucuronide** (active/therapeutic, ~2× morphine, renally eliminated → accumulates in renal failure) and **M3-glucuronide** (neuroexcitatory); rapid onset IV, duration 3-4h. Not recommended as an entry drug.

segments.label: Oxycodone
segments.value:
**μ + κ** agonist → significant spinal analgesia; more lipophilic than morphine; controlled release; **+ naltrexone** (↓ constipation).
• **Fentanyl/fentanyls** (remifentanil, alfentanil, sufentanil): **high potency, extreme lipophilicity**; **metabolism via CYP3A4** (to norfentanyl → interactions with 3A4 inhibitors/inducers); transmucosal preparations (15-30 min); ⚠️ leading cause of overdose deaths in the US.
• **Buprenorphine** (Temgesic): **μ partial agonist + κ antagonist** → ↑ supraspinal analgesia; **reduced/slower respiratory depression**; used in **detoxification** (replaces methadone); **+ naloxone = Suboxone** (prevents IV abuse).

segments.label: Methadone
segments.value: oral; ⚠️ **long and variable half-life** → accumulation, **QT prolongation, torsade de pointes**, sleep apnea; suboptimal bioavailability; **hepatic metabolism by N-demethylation (CYP3A4 substrate** → interactions with 3A4 inducers/inhibitors that alter the QT); used in detoxification.

segments.label: Hydromorphone
segments.value: usable in **renal failure** (inactive metabolite).

segments.label: Tapentadol
segments.value: latest molecule; **pure MOP agonist (-30%) + NA reuptake inhibitor** (no 5-HT → ⚠️ **NO serotonin syndrome**, can be combined with SSRIs/TCAs); non-racemic/non-prodrug → usable in **hepatic failure**; no CYP interaction; intrinsic sparing effect; less nausea/vomiting/constipation/dependence; lipophilic.

entries.name: Antagonists, paracetamol and cannabinoids
entries.memo: **Naloxone** reopens the μ receptors within minutes, but it is short-acting → re-observe the patient after awakening.

segments.label: Naloxone
segments.value: antagonist, **parenteral**, latency ~30 sec (immediate), very high affinity for μ → displaces heroin → **LIFESAVER in overdose** (does not work orally → used in Suboxone).

segments.label: Naltrexone
segments.value: oral, longer latency; + oxycodone (↓ constipation).

segments.label: Loperamide
segments.value: MOP agonist that is **not lipophilic** (does not cross the BBB) → **antidiarrheal**.

segments.label: Paracetamol
segments.value: inhibits **central COX** + ↑ **cannabinoidergic (CB1)** and opioidergic/serotonergic transmission; analgesic/antipyretic, poor anti-inflammatory; ⚠️ **hepatotoxic** (max 3-4 g/day), not gastric-injuring/not nephrotoxic; drug of abuse in chronic use. *(See file A5.)*

segments.label: Cannabinoids
segments.value: analgesia via **CB1** on the GABAergic interneurons of the nucleus raphe magnus. *(See file B7.)*
